data.title: Write RED tests for scan-docs.sh file-type guard behavior
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Context
This task creates the test file for the new scan-docs.sh helper script (Proposal B). Per Behavioral Testing Standard Rule 1, check existing coverage first — no existing test covers scan-docs.sh. Per Rule 2, tests assert observable behavior: exit code and stderr log output, not file content.

## What to Build
Create `tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh` with bash tests asserting file-type guard behavior of scan-docs.sh:

1. test_binary_file_skipped: Given a directory with a binary file (created via `printf '\x00\x01\x02'`), when scan-docs.sh is called with that dir, then exit 0 and stderr contains "SKIP:binary"
2. test_large_file_skipped: Given a directory with a text file >200KB (created via `dd if=/dev/urandom bs=201k count=1 | base64`), when scan-docs.sh is called with that dir, then exit 0 and stderr contains "SKIP:size"
3. test_path_traversal_rejected: Given a --doc-folder path containing "../", when scan-docs.sh validates it, then exit 1 and stderr contains "ERROR:path_traversal"
4. test_nonexistent_folder_rejected: Given a path to a nonexistent directory, when scan-docs.sh is called, then exit 1 and stderr contains "ERROR:not_found"
5. test_empty_folder_returns_empty_facts: Given an empty directory, when scan-docs.sh is called, then exit 0 and stdout is valid JSON: {"facts":[],"skipped":[]}

These tests must FAIL (RED) until scan-docs.sh is implemented. Add .test-index entry mapping scan-docs.sh to the new test file.

## TDD Requirement
These tests run RED before Task T2 (implement scan-docs.sh file-type guard). Run with: `bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh`

## Test Approach
- Given: a temp dir with specific file types (binary, large, text)
- When: scan-docs.sh is invoked with the temp dir path
- Then: exit code and stderr log messages match expected behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh` exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh
- [ ] Test file contains all 5 named test functions
  Verify: grep -c 'test_binary_file_skipped\|test_large_file_skipped\|test_path_traversal_rejected\|test_nonexistent_folder_rejected\|test_empty_folder_returns_empty_facts' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 5)}'
- [ ] Running the test file exits non-zero (RED — scan-docs.sh does not exist yet)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] `.test-index` updated with RED marker [test_binary_file_skipped] for plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh
  Verify: grep '\[test_binary_file_skipped\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] `.test-index` entry maps plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh to tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh (fuzzy match check: normalized 'scandocssh' is substring of 'testonboardingscandocssh' — passes)
  Verify: grep 'plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
